title: Instructional Resources: Instructional Materials
description: Conroe ISD follows a defined process for selecting the textbooks, digital resources, and other learning materials used in classrooms. The district prioritizes materials that align with state curriculum standards and support student learning. Parents and community members can find information about how these materials are reviewed and approved, helping ensure transparency in what students are taught.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's Policy EFA significantly expands the definition of instructional materials, adds detailed criteria for selection (including civic knowledge requirements and founding documents), establishes a new "Rejected Instructional Materials" section prohibiting use of State Board of Education-rejected materials, strengthens the Superintendent's compliance certification obligation, and refines the informal/formal reconsideration process language.

An appreciation of: i. The importance and responsibility of participating in civic life; ii. A commitment to the United States and its form of government; and iii. A commitment to free speech and civil discourse.",
  "When selecting Instructional Materials or providing instruction regarding the founding documents of the United States, these shall be presented appropriately for the grade level and in supportive, comprehensive, and affirmatory manner that respects the entirety of the document. The founding documents of the United States include the entirety of the Declaration of Independence; the entirety of the United States Constitution; the Federalist Papers, including the entirety of Essays 10 and 51; excerpts from Alexis de Tocqueville's Democracy in America; the transcript of the first Lincoln-Douglas debate; the writings of the founding fathers of the United States; the entirety of Frederick Douglass's speeches "The Meaning of July Fourth for the Negro" and "What the Black Man Wants"; and the entirety of Martin Luther King Jr.'s speech "I Have a Dream."",

  "Any materials or Instructional Materials adopting, supporting, or promoting on the list of rejected instructional materials maintained by the State Board of Education under Texas Education Code § 31.022(a) shall be removed from any District premises generally accessible to the student population, including but not limited to any library or classroom setting.",

timeline_local.notes: The document is a clear redline with red underline = additions and red strikethrough = removals. The cover page title reads "Discuss and Possibly Adopt Revisions to Board Policy EFA (Local)" which maps to action = 'adopted' per the provided rules. The new 'Rejected Instructional Materials' and 'Superintendent Authority' section headings are themselves red-underlined (new). Item numbering in the selection criteria list was also reorganized (old item 7 removed; new items 5, 6, 8, 9, 10 added; old unnumbered items 1–4 appear largely unchanged in black). Some capitalization corrections (iInstructional mMaterials → Instructional Materials) appear throughout but are treated as part of larger changed passages rather than standalone items.

description_local: This policy governs how the school district selects and approves the textbooks, digital resources, and other learning materials used in classrooms. It applies to all students in the district, as well as the teachers and administrators responsible for choosing and using those materials. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it outlines the process by which decisions are made about what educational content students are exposed to, and it may include opportunities for public input or review before materials are formally adopted.
